title: NOVO NORDISK / HIMS & HERS GLP-1 PARTNERSHIP
headline: Novo Nordisk and Hims & Hers end legal dispute, agree to sell branded GLP-1 drugs together through telehealth platform.
key_facts: Partnership announced March 9, 2026; Deal resolves prior conflict over compounded GLP-1 medications; Hims & Hers agrees to "limit compounding" as part of settlement; Will now distribute Novo's *branded* GLP-1 drugs (likely Ozempic/Wegovy) via telehealth; No financial terms disclosed

title: FDA EXPANDS BMS SOTYKTU LABEL TO PSORIATIC ARTHRITIS
headline: Bristol Myers Squibb's Sotyktu (deucravacitinib) wins FDA approval for psoriatic arthritis indication.
key_facts: FDA approval announced March 9, 2026, 11:16am; Drug name: Sotyktu (deucravacitinib); New indication: psoriatic arthritis (PsA); Original approval: psoriasis; This was a **tracked PDUFA date from March 6 episode** (outcome confirmed)

title: BRISTOL MYERS CELMOD BLOOD CANCER SUCCESS
headline: Bristol Myers claims Phase 3 success for second-generation CELMoD drug in blood cancer.
key_facts: Phase 3 study met primary endpoint (announced March 9); Drug class: CELMoD (cereblon E3 ligase modulator) - next-generation after lenalidomide/revlimid; Indication: blood cancer (likely multiple myeloma based on context); No specific efficacy data disclosed yet; Second CELMoD success for BMS (follow

title: INCYTE RECEIVES FDA CRL DUE TO NOVO INDIANA FACILITY
headline: Incyte becomes third company to receive Complete Response Letter over manufacturing issues at Novo Nordisk's Indiana fill-finish plant.
key_facts: CRL (Complete Response Letter) issued by FDA March 9, 2026; Issue: manufacturing problems at **Novo Nordisk's Indiana facility**; Incyte drug affected: PD-1 inhibitor (checkpoint inhibitor, oncology); Third company impacted by same facility issues (other two not named in summaries); Not related to d

title: IPSEN WITHDRAWS TAZVERIK CANCER DRUG
headline: Ipsen to withdraw Tazverik from market due to secondary malignancy safety signals in confirmatory study.
key_facts: Voluntary market withdrawal announced March 9, 2026; Drug: Tazverik (tazemetostat) - EZH2 inhibitor; Safety issue: **secondary malignancies** (new cancers) detected in post-approval confirmatory study; Tazverik acquired via Ipsen's purchase of **Epizyme** (prior acquisition); Indication: epithelioid

title: XENON PHASE 3 WIN IN EPILEPSY
headline: Xenon Pharmaceuticals achieves Phase 3 success with epilepsy drug, fueling blockbuster hopes for first commercial launch.
key_facts: Phase 3 study met primary endpoint (announced March 9); Indication: epilepsy; First product launch anticipated for Xenon; Analysts citing "blockbuster hopes" (>$1B annual revenue potential implied); No specific efficacy data disclosed

title: UNIVERSAL HEALTH SERVICES ACQUIRES TALKSPACE FOR $835M
headline: Universal Health Services (UHS) acquires telehealth mental health platform Talkspace for $835 million.
key_facts: Deal value: **$835 million**; Buyer: Universal Health Services (UHS) - major hospital/facilities operator; Target: Talkspace - digital mental health/teletherapy platform; Deal announced March 9, 2026; Talkspace claims "1.2 million members" per separate reference

title: MINERALYS THERAPEUTICS NDA ACCEPTED, OSA TRIAL DATA
headline: FDA accepts Mineralys NDA for lorundrostat in hypertension; PDUFA date December 22, 2026; separate obstructive sleep apnea trial reports topline results.
key_facts: NDA acceptance announced March 9, 2026, 4:30pm ET; Drug: lorundrostat; Indication: hypertension in adults; PDUFA target action date: December 22, 2026**; Separate trial: Explore-OSA (obstructive sleep apnea) - topline results released (no efficacy details in summary); Mechanism: aldosterone synthase

title: TELIX PROSTATE CANCER PHASE 3 SUCCESS
headline: Telix Pharmaceuticals' ProstACT Global Phase 3 study (Part 1) achieves primary objectives in prostate cancer.
key_facts: Study name: ProstACT Global Phase 3 (Part 1); Primary objectives: **achieved**; Indication: prostate cancer (likely radiopharmaceutical therapy based on Telix's portfolio); Conference call held March 10, 9:30am AEDT (March 9, 6:30pm EDT); No specific efficacy metrics disclosed

title: DIANTHUS $400M PUBLIC OFFERING
headline: Dianthus Therapeutics announces proposed $400 million underwritten public offering.
key_facts: Offering size: **$400 million** (proposed); Type: underwritten public offering; Announced March 9, 4:01pm ET; Nasdaq ticker: DNTH

title: ELI LILLY CEO 2025 COMPENSATION
headline: Eli Lilly CEO David Ricks received $36.7 million pay package for 2025.
key_facts: Executive: David Ricks, CEO; Compensation: **$36.7 million** total for 2025; Disclosed March 9, 3:20pm; Lilly ticker: LLY currently trading at $1,008.39 (up 1.82% on day per Yahoo Finance); Lilly YTD 2026 performance: **-6.17%** (Yahoo Finance data)
